#+title: External knowledge
#+description: Reference docs about external systems and domain concepts ORE Studio does not own — libraries, file formats, and platform features.
#+type: knowledge
#+version: 2
#+level: cross
#+filetags: :knowledge:external:index:
#+created: 2026-05-21
#+updated: 2026-05-21

Reference material about systems and concepts ORE Studio /uses but
does not own/. The rule of thumb: if the doc would exist (in roughly
the same form) even if ORE Studio did not, it lives here. Docs about
/how we use/ an external system stay in the [[id:7474935C-95D1-3034-B833-83DCD9FB3A09][knowledge root]] — for
example, =ORE Model Configuration= captures /our/ patterns, even
though it references [[id:1cbdea40-5fae-4f04-bd21-2bb29172b5aa][ORE]].

* Risk and pricing libraries

- [[id:1cbdea40-5fae-4f04-bd21-2bb29172b5aa][Open Source Risk Engine (ORE)]] — the upstream risk-analytics
  library ORE Studio wraps.
- [[id:0412444a-0a4c-4611-887c-09353a3cb253][QuantLib]] — the open-source quantitative-finance library ORE
  itself builds on.

* File formats and protocols

- [[id:FCC3BBA5-DFA3-17F4-69B3-B7958C05B1F8][Financial Products Markup Language (FpML)]] — industry XML
  standard for representing financial products.
- [[id:1A95DFA1-932C-B094-4653-BFCAD266C2AD][Coding schemes]] — FpML mechanism for restricting fields to a
  known set of values.

* Domain concepts

- [[id:0929B047-1A31-3514-9EDB-EF7B4F012C5D][Standing settlement instructions]] — what SSIs are and how they
  flow through the trading lifecycle.

* Platform features

- [[id:B28747DF-A735-1C84-4E43-11ADFA8B05E3][PostgreSQL Row-Level Security]] — RLS at the database level. Our
  application of it is described in [[id:B7E9F3A1-C8D2-4E6B-A1F5-9D3C7E8B2A4F][ores.sql]].
- [[id:A1C3E5F7-92B4-4D6A-B8C2-3F1D7E9A0B5C][Shared library symbol visibility]] — visibility annotations and
  hidden-by-default at the compiler/linker level.

* See also

- [[id:7474935C-95D1-3034-B833-83DCD9FB3A09][Knowledge]] — the cross-cutting index this folder hangs off.
